creator: dewayne.geraghty@heuristicsystems.com.au
text:
Ryan,  Thanks for looking into this.  Platform is 9.2 Stable (rebuilt
each week), and I update /usr/ports via svn before a ports rebuild. 
This is repeatable on both i386 and amd64.  Bear in mind that I remove
$STAGING and $WRKDIRPREFIX  before a full rebuild; along with pkg_delete
-a and anything else I can think of so I have an almost virgin setup
(but not my ccache).

Default build (which is what I'm testing and documenting below)
# make __MAKE_CONF=/dev/null showconfig | grep "=on"
     PERL=on: Perl scripting language support
     USER=on: Run as user freeradius, group freeradius

Options that I'm wanting to use use,
# make showconfig|grep "=on"
     HEIMDAL=on: With Heimdal Kerberos support
     HEIMDAL_PORT=on: With Heimdal Kerberos from ports
     KERBEROS=on: Kerberos support
     LDAP=on: LDAP support
     SSL_PORT=on: Use OpenSSL from the ports collection
     USER=on: Run as user freeradius, group freeradius

After removing perl/example.pl from pkg-plist,  this  "make package"
command
make -DBATCH  __MAKE_CONF=/dev/null clean deinstall ; make -DBATCH
__MAKE_CONF=/dev/null package
is used, so there's effectively no make.conf involvement, resulting in:

===>  Building package for freeradius3-3.0.1_2
tar:
share/examples/freeradius/raddb/mods-config/attr_filter/access_challenge: Cannot
stat: No such file or directory

Cannot stat: No such file or directory

Regards, Dewayne.
